# Practice-Section Source Catalogue

This file records public repositories and bibliographic items cited in the book practice sections. It is a practice-section source catalogue, not the full bibliography of the book manuscript.

## Chapters 3--4: POT — Python Optimal Transport

- Repository: https://github.com/PythonOT/POT
- Reference: Flamary et al. (2021), *POT: Python Optimal Transport*, Journal of Machine Learning Research.

## Chapter 5: Sinkhorn and entropic optimal transport

- Reference: Cuturi (2013), *Sinkhorn Distances: Lightspeed Computation of Optimal Transport*, NeurIPS.
- Reference: Feydy et al. (2019), *Interpolating between Optimal Transport and MMD using Sinkhorn Divergences*, AISTATS.
- Reference: Peyré and Cuturi (2019), *Computational Optimal Transport*, Foundations and Trends in Machine Learning.

## Chapter 6: Learned Primal-Dual and Wasserstein-loss inverse problems

- Repository: https://github.com/adler-j/learned_primal_dual
- Reference: Adler and Öktem (2018), *Learned Primal-Dual Reconstruction*, IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging.
- Repository: https://github.com/adler-j/wasserstein_inverse_problems
- Reference: Adler, Ringh, Öktem, and Karlsson (2018), *Learning to solve inverse problems using Wasserstein loss*.

## Chapter 7: OT-CycleGAN

- Repository: https://github.com/jryoungw/OT_CycleGAN
- Reference: Sim, Oh, Kim, Jung, and Ye (2020), *Optimal Transport driven CycleGAN for Unsupervised Learning in Inverse Problems*, SIAM Journal on Imaging Sciences.

## Chapter 8: ADFWI

- Repository: https://github.com/liufeng2317/ADFWI
- Reference: Liu, Li, Zou, and Li (2025), *Automatic Differentiation-Based Full Waveform Inversion With Flexible Workflows*, Journal of Geophysical Research: Machine Learning and Computation.

## Chapter 9: OT-Flow

- Repository: https://github.com/EmoryMLIP/OT-Flow
- Reference: Onken, Fung, Li, and Ruthotto (2021), *OT-Flow: Fast and Accurate Continuous Normalizing Flows via Optimal Transport*, AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence.

## Chapter 10: Flower and pyABC

- Repository: https://github.com/mehrsapo/Flower
- Reference: Pourya, El Rawas, and Unser (2025), *FLOWER: A Flow-Matching Solver for Inverse Problems*.
- Repository: https://github.com/ICB-DCM/pyABC
- Reference: Klinger et al. (2018), *pyABC: distributed, likelihood-free inference*, Bioinformatics.
